feedSummary.title: Live Policy Feed
feedSummary.body: Policy breadth is still narrow. The refresh screened 21 public-policy rows, but only 1 survived the soy/procurement relevance filter, with the visible anchor coming from Commerce Department.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Translate current legislation flow into procurement, pricing, compliance, and sourcing impact for rapid buyer decisions.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Policy matters operationally only when it changes timing, cost, compliance burden, or supply-chain friction.
quantResearchProtocol: Rank items by operational transmission speed and enforcement likelihood.
Separate confirmed action, proposed action, and administrative noise.
Classify each top item as immediate, near-term, or deferred risk.
Map each risk to procurement timing and execution consequences.
inferenceConstraints: Do not provide political, partisan, or ideological commentary.
Do not use doom language, hype language, or speculative certainty.
Do not issue urgent posture without implementation evidence.
outputRequirements: State top operational risks and impact horizon.
Provide one escalation trigger to monitor.

sourceRecords.recordHint: Farm, Food, and National Security Act of 2026

notes: The card ranks the visible item set but also states the screened universe size so thin breadth is not mistaken for full coverage.
Policy can be lead-driver status even when the live item set is concentrated because the macro uncertainty series is currently extreme.

sourcePressure.title: Source Activity
sourcePressure.body: Source breadth is concentrated. Commerce Department supplies 1 of the 1 visible items, so corroboration is adequate for watch status but still too narrow for high-conviction shock framing.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Assess whether source concentration supports confidence or increases interpretation risk for procurement decisions.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Narrow source breadth increases policy-read error risk unless corroborated by independent channels.
quantResearchProtocol: Measure lead-source share and breadth across active items.
Detect source-dominance shifts against recent baseline.
Flag corroboration gaps in high-impact lanes.
Map source structure into confidence posture.
inferenceConstraints: Do not overstate confidence when source breadth is narrow.
Do not reduce reliability analysis to raw counts alone.
Do not blur confirmed evidence with unconfirmed narrative.
outputRequirements: State concentration regime and confidence effect.
Explain operational consequence of low corroboration.

sourceRecords.recordHint: three Agriculture Department-led current items in visible policy set
one congressional anchor item in visible policy set

notes: Source concentration math is explicit inside the card.
Confidence is tied to corroboration breadth, not just raw item relevance.

tagPressure.title: Policy Tag Pressure
tagPressure.body: Policy-theme pressure currently clusters in palm, policy, tariff, which points more to trade and compliance monitoring than to an immediate soybean-oil rule shock.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Identify policy themes most likely to propagate into procurement pressure and compliance workload.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Theme concentration is only actionable when linked to concrete operational transmission channels.
quantResearchProtocol: Rank tags by frequency, persistence, and transmission relevance.
Separate trade, compliance, energy, and agriculture theme lanes.
Differentiate structural pressure from temporary bursts.
Map top themes to buyer watch priorities.
inferenceConstraints: Do not equate high frequency with high impact without context.
Do not collapse distinct theme channels into one generic category.
Do not claim certainty without implementation detail.
outputRequirements: Report top themes with impact horizon.
Provide concrete watchlist priorities tied to operations.

sourceRecords.recordHint: USDA and Commerce items used for trade, policy, and agriculture tag counts
farm-bill anchor item used for macro and policy tag count

notes: Theme ranking is based on the visible current item set rather than a raw feed dump.
The current taxonomy is informative but still too administrative to justify rule-shock language by itself.

items.title: Foreign-Trade Zone 102; Application for Subzone; BASF Agricultural Solutions US LLC; Fenton and Palmyra, Missouri
